scenario: Lobsters work session - audit the Story contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer contains zero orientation: it never names the project (Lobsters), never describes the Rails directory layout (app/models, app/controllers, app/mailboxes, lib/extras), and never locates the Story model as the central submission object. An agent reading this still has no map and must explore from scratch.

specificity.rationale: File paths are technically present (app/models/vote.rb:181, app/models/story.rb:693) but they answer a completely different question (vote recalculation mechanics), not the orientation question, so their specificity provides no value to the downstream task.

justification.rationale: The answer never explains why Story is the central submission model that comments, votes, tags, and flags hang off of; no architectural rationale for the project's shape is given.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty flags are raised, but more critically the answer never engages with the orientation question at all, so there is nothing to flag uncertainty about regarding the project structure.

relationship.rationale: The only relationship mentioned is the polymorphic dispatch between Comment and Story via vote.rb:185 `target`, but Story's associations to comments/votes/tags/flags as the central model are entirely absent.

steps.step: Map the Story contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps only the scoring sub-machinery (update_score_and_recalculate! at story.rb:693-718, calculated_hotness at story.rb:550) with file:line, but completely omits what Step 2 asks for: the concern Story includes (the Token concern that supplies short-URL tokens), the associations other records hang off Story through (comments, votes, taggings), and what the overall contract means. An agent would still need a fresh exploration of story.rb's header and schema to find those pieces.

specificity.rationale: The scoring path is cited concretely (story.rb:693-718, story.rb:550-580, vote.rb:181/185), but the concern name, its file:line, and the association declarations that form the contract are entirely absent.

justification.rationale: The answer explains what calculated_hotness computes (tag hotness_mod, author bump, log-scaled age decay) but never explains WHY the Story contract is shaped the way it is — why a concern is used for token generation, why associations hang off Story rather than the reverse.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere; the answer presents all its claims as confirmed without acknowledging the large portions of the contract (concern, associations) it did not examine.

relationship.rationale: Within the vote path the answer traces the polymorphic dispatch (vote.target → Story or Comment) correctly, but the binding relationships the rubric asks for — which concern supplies what behavior to Story, which associations hang off Story and how — are entirely absent.

steps.step: Trace how a story is created and ranked

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the vote recalculation path (vote.rb:127 → target → update_score_and_recalculate!) but completely omits the story creation chain: no StoriesController#create, no URL/link fetching or validation, no markdown rendering pipeline. An agent tasked with changing how a submission becomes a stored Story would need a full fresh exploration to find the controller, URL, and Markdowner layers.

specificity.rationale: File:line citations are concrete for what is covered (vote.rb:181, story.rb:693–718, calculated_hotness at story.rb:550), but the required pieces — the stories controller create action, the URL/link handler, and the Markdowner — are entirely absent, not vaguely named.

justification.rationale: The hotness formula rationale is present (tag hotness_mod, author bump, capped comment points, log-scale by sign, age penalty via HOTNESS_WINDOW), but no justification is given for the creation path, URL handling, or markdown rendering because those layers are not traced at all.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer never flags that it skipped the controller create path, URL fetching, and markdown rendering — the three layers the prompt explicitly requires — presenting a partial vote-recalc answer as if it were a complete trace of submission creation and ranking.

relationship.rationale: An explicit chain is assembled within the vote path (vote_thusly → target resolution → update_score_and_recalculate! → calculated_hotness), but the requested chain — submission → controller → URL/markdown → score — is entirely absent, so the relationship criterion for this step is not met.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Story contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps only one narrow slice of voting/flagging (the score-recalculation pipeline in vote.rb/story.rb/comment.rb) and omits five of the six required areas entirely: content and rendering, search, statistics, inbound email, and moderation are not mentioned at all. An agent reading this cannot start reworking the Story contract safely — it would need a full fresh grep sweep to find the search indexer, stats cache, inbox mailbox, markdowner, and moderation controllers.

specificity.rationale: Within the narrow vote/score path the citations are precise (app/models/vote.rb:181, story.rb:693-718, spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82), but the five unaddressed areas produce zero file:line entries, so the overall specificity of the audit is very low.

justification.rationale: The answer explains WHY the score/hotness recalculation is shaped the way it is (Wilson confidence, log-scaled hotness, raw-SQL re-derivation), but offers no explanation for why any of the missing areas (search, stats, email, moderation, rendering) are dependents — because those areas are absent.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no uncertainty and gives no acknowledgment that entire dependency areas (mailbox, search indexer, stats cache, moderation controllers, markdowner) were not examined; the agent would have no signal that re-exploration is needed.

relationship.rationale: The polymorphic dispatch path (Vote#target → Story or Comment → update_score_and_recalculate!) is stated as an explicit chain, which is good for the one area covered. But the rubric specifically calls out the non-obvious concern/association/derive routes for search, stats, mailbox, and markdowner — none of those connecting relationships appear because those dependents are entirely missing.

steps.step: Trace how story identity and visibility are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps vote recalculation and score/hotness computation paths (vote.rb:127-181, story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363), not the presence/visibility guards the step asks for in search, stats, inbound-email, and moderation paths. None of the asked-about areas are mentioned.

specificity.rationale: File:line citations are abundant but applied to entirely wrong code (vote scoring, calculated_hotness, confidence_order); no concrete guard method or file:line is given for any of the four asked-about paths.

justification.rationale: The answer never explains why work must re-confirm a story is real or visible (e.g., it may have been deleted or hidden between enqueue and execution); the WHY for guards is entirely absent.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no gaps or implicit guards because it does not engage with the guard question at all; its 'regression_risk' note is scoped to vote scoring SQL, not visibility checks.

relationship.rationale: No path is stated connecting any guard to any dependent (search indexer, stats cache, mailbox, moderation controller); the answer names only vote-to-score relationships, which are off-topic for this step.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces only the voting/score-recalculation pipeline (vote.rb→story.rb/comment.rb) at an implementation-internals level; the rubric demands coverage across rendering, search, stats, email, and moderation — none of which appear. A risk ranking across areas is entirely absent, so the agent would still need a fresh exploration of every other dependent zone.

specificity.rationale: Within its narrow scope the citations are precise (story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363, vote.rb:181), but most at-risk dependents are simply missing from the answer, so the file:line density cannot offset the coverage gap.

justification.rationale: The answer explains the mechanics of score/hotness/confidence recomputation well, but offers no justification for why other dependent areas (search indexer, stats cache, mailbox, moderation controllers) are high or low risk — they are not mentioned at all.

uncertainty.rationale: There is no acknowledgment that the scope covered is partial or that indirect dependents (concern-reached, association-reached, or derive-reached) may be missing; the answer reads as complete when it is not.

relationship.rationale: The vote→target→Story/Comment dispatch chain is stated explicitly, which is correct for the narrow voting path; but the broader dependent graph (search via association, stats via derive, email via parent-story lookup) is absent, so the relationship criterion is satisfied only for one of the six required areas.

map_quality.rationale: The answer produces a detailed, actionable map for the vote/recalculation subsystem (vote.rb:181, comment.rb:339-363, story.rb:693-718) with specs grouped by area, but it entirely omits the broader Story contract dependents the scenario demanded: search (app/models/search.rb), stats/graph cache, inbox_mailbox.rb, moderation controllers, the markdowner, and the link-URL handler. 'Every dependent grouped by area' is the requirement; only the voting/flagging area is covered.

specificity.rationale: What IS included is exceptionally concrete: exact file:line for both polymorphic implementations, column lists written by each raw SQL UPDATE, spec line ranges (e.g. spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82, story_spec.rb:373-401), and the single call-site to preserve. An agent could act on the covered slice without any follow-up lookup.

justification.rationale: The answer explains WHY the call site matters, WHY two implementations exist, and WHY the SQL re-derivation makes value-method tests the regression guard. However it offers no justification for excluding search/stats/mailbox/moderation from the map, leaving an agent uncertain whether those areas were deliberately scoped out or simply missed.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer notes the polymorphic dispatch complication and the SQL vs in-memory delta invariant, but never flags that its coverage of dependents may be incomplete. A correct uncertainty signal would acknowledge that the search-indexer, stats-cache, and mailbox paths were not verified and need separate review.

relationship.rationale: For the recalc chain the relationships are explicit and correct: vote_thusly → vote.rb:177 target resolution → vote.rb:185 polymorphic dispatch → Comment/Story implementations → value methods. But the Story contract change also reaches search/stats/mailbox/moderation through associations and concerns, and none of those connecting paths appear in the map.
